title: Aetheolaena loeseneri
intro: Aetheolaena loeseneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a member of the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Aetheolaena loeseneri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aetheolaena loeseneri is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine as a tincture or extract.

This plant is further classified within the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. It is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically within the kingdom Plantae.
